name: 12-retry-on-429
description: Canonical scenario #12: retry on 429. PARITY FINDING (ambiguity call: documented divergence) — the Anthropic SDK (via the spawned `claude` subprocess) retries on HTTP 429 with the Retry-After header, observable as `system:api_retry` messages on the transcript. The OR /responses SDK does NOT retry 429 by default: `betaResponsesSend` ships with `retryCodes: ['5XX']` (see node_modules/@openrouter/sdk/.../betaResponsesSend.js); the agent surface in src/agent.ts does not expose an override knob, so a 429 thrown from /responses surfaces as an error event to the host. Since this card's hard rule forbids src/ changes, v1 of scenario #12 scripts the Anthropic side with a 429-then-success sequence (turn 0 = failure(429), turn 1 = success — both at the same promptHash since the SDK retries an identical request body) and scripts the OR side with a single success entry (no 429, since the SDK can't reach a comparable terminal otherwise). The parity claim asserted: BOTH sides project to identical canonical event streams once the Anthropic-side retry completes — proving the 429+retry is invisible at the canonical event layer. The retry itself is verified by the supplemental test in scenarios.comparative.test.ts which counts `system:api_retry` messages on the Anthropic transcript. Follow-up needed: expose `retryCodes` on `OpenRouterAgentRunOptions` so the OR side can retry 429 too — that's a src/agent.ts change, out of scope here.
prompt: Tell me one thing about TypeScript.
systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer concisely.

content.text: TypeScript is a typed superset of JavaScript.
TypeScript is a typed superset of JavaScript.
